**Westminster Theological Seminary** (**WTS**) is a [Protestant](/wiki/Protestantism "Protestantism") [theological](/wiki/Christian_theology "Christian theology") [seminary](/wiki/Seminary "Seminary") in the [Reformed theological tradition](/wiki/Reformed_theology "Reformed theology") in [Glenside, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Glenside,_Pennsylvania "Glenside, Pennsylvania"). It was founded by members of the faculty of [Princeton Theological Seminary](/wiki/Princeton_Theological_Seminary "Princeton Theological Seminary") in 1929 after Princeton chose to take a liberal direction during the [Fundamentalist–Modernist controversy](/wiki/Fundamentalist%E2%80%93Modernist_controversy "Fundamentalist–Modernist controversy").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Westminster_Theological_Seminary&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/34/Machen_Hall%2C_Westminster_Theological_Seminary%2C_Glenside_PA_04.JPG/330px-Machen_Hall%2C_Westminster_Theological_Seminary%2C_Glenside_PA_04.JPG)](/wiki/File:Machen_Hall,_Westminster_Theological_Seminary,_Glenside_PA_04.JPG)

J. Gresham Machen Memorial Hall

Westminster Theological Seminary was formed in 1929, largely under the leadership and funding of [J. Gresham Machen](/wiki/J._Gresham_Machen "J. Gresham Machen").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Though independent, it has a close relationship with the [Orthodox Presbyterian Church](/wiki/Orthodox_Presbyterian_Church "Orthodox Presbyterian Church"), which Machen helped found in 1936. The seminary was founded by members of the faculty of [Princeton Theological Seminary](/wiki/Princeton_Theological_Seminary "Princeton Theological Seminary"), following a controversy over the liberal direction that Princeton was beginning to take.[[4]](#cite_note-History-4) Westminster Theological Seminary considers itself to be the faithful continuation of Princeton's historic theological tradition. Many of the founders of Westminster, including Machen, [John Murray](/wiki/John_Murray_(theologian) "John Murray (theologian)"), [Oswald Allis](/wiki/Oswald_Allis "Oswald Allis"), [Robert Dick Wilson](/wiki/Robert_Dick_Wilson "Robert Dick Wilson"), and [Cornelius Van Til](/wiki/Cornelius_Van_Til "Cornelius Van Til"), had been professors at Princeton prior to the controversy.[[4]](#cite_note-History-4) The first president of the seminary was [Edmund Clowney](/wiki/Edmund_Clowney "Edmund Clowney"), who served from 1966 until 1984. He was followed by [George C. Fuller](/wiki/George_C._Fuller "George C. Fuller") and [Samuel T. Logan](/wiki/Samuel_T._Logan "Samuel T. Logan"). The current president is [Peter Lillback](/wiki/Peter_Lillback "Peter Lillback"), who also serves as a professor of historical theology.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Westminster_Theological_Seminary%2C_Glenside_PA_05.JPG/330px-Westminster_Theological_Seminary%2C_Glenside_PA_05.JPG)](/wiki/File:Westminster_Theological_Seminary,_Glenside_PA_05.JPG)

Campus in 2013

In 1982, the [California](/wiki/California "California") branch of Westminster became an independent institution, the [Westminster Seminary California](/wiki/Westminster_Seminary_California "Westminster Seminary California"), and in 2009 the [Dallas](/wiki/Dallas "Dallas") branch was established as Redeemer Theological Seminary and since 2017 has been [Reformed Theological Seminary](/wiki/Reformed_Theological_Seminary "Reformed Theological Seminary")'s Dallas campus.

In 2011, Westminster signed a [Memorandum of Understanding](/wiki/Memorandum_of_understanding "Memorandum of understanding") with International Reformed Evangelical Seminary in [Jakarta, Indonesia](/wiki/Jakarta,_Indonesia "Jakarta, Indonesia"). The two schools have been publishing the journals: International Journal of Reformed Theology and Life and Unio Cum Christo.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Westminster also created an [endowment](/wiki/Endowed_chair "Endowed chair") for the [Stephen Tong](/wiki/Stephen_Tong "Stephen Tong") Chair of Reformed Theology the same year.[[7]](#cite_note-wtsprofessor-7)

## Theological position

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Westminster_Theological_Seminary&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Theological position")]

In [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia") on September 25, 1929, J. Gresham Machen declared the following in his inaugural address:

> "We believe, first, that the Christian religion, as set forth in the Confession of Faith of the Presbyterian Church, is true; we believe, second, that the Christian religion welcomes and is capable of scholarly defense; and we believe, third, that the Christian religion should be proclaimed without fear or favor, and in clear opposition to whatever opposes it, whether from within or without the church, as the only way of salvation for lost mankind. On that platform, brethren, we stand. Pray that we may be enabled by God’s grace to stand firm. Pray that the students who go forth from Westminster Theological Seminary may know Christ as their own Savior and may proclaim to others the gospel of his love."[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3f/Montgomery_Library%2C_Westminster_Theological_Seminary%2C_Glenside_PA_02.JPG/250px-Montgomery_Library%2C_Westminster_Theological_Seminary%2C_Glenside_PA_02.JPG)](/wiki/File:Montgomery_Library,_Westminster_Theological_Seminary,_Glenside_PA_02.JPG)

Entrance of Montgomery Library

The current board and faculty continue to hold to this original vision. All trustees and faculty members are required to affirm their agreement with the theological perspective presented in the [Westminster Confession of Faith](/wiki/Westminster_Confession_of_Faith "Westminster Confession of Faith") and the [Larger](/wiki/Westminster_Larger_Catechism "Westminster Larger Catechism") and [Shorter Catechisms](/wiki/Westminster_Shorter_Catechism "Westminster Shorter Catechism"), the core doctrinal statements of many [Presbyterian](/wiki/Presbyterian "Presbyterian") denominations.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Westminster's strict adherence to the [Westminster Standards](/wiki/Westminster_Standards "Westminster Standards") and to Protestant theology in general has led to several dismissals of tenured faculty members since 1980. In 1981, theology professor [Norman Shepherd](/wiki/Norman_Shepherd "Norman Shepherd") was dismissed from Westminster due to his views on [the doctrine of justification](/wiki/Justification_(theology) "Justification (theology)") by faith alone.[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 2008, Old Testament professor [Peter Enns](/wiki/Peter_Enns "Peter Enns") was dismissed from Westminster over controversial views expressed in his book *Inspiration and Incarnation: Evangelicals and the Problem of the Old Testament*,[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) and in 2014 Old Testament professor Douglas Green was terminated from his position over his views on the relationship between the Old Testament and the New Testament.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In the early 1990s, [Middle States Association of Colleges and Schools](/wiki/Middle_States_Association_of_Colleges_and_Schools "Middle States Association of Colleges and Schools") threatened Westminster's accreditation because of the seminary's refusal to give women membership on its board of trustees.[[14]](#cite_note-14) The accrediting body backed down after the [United States Department of Education](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Education "United States Department of Education") took the seminary's side in the dispute.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Accreditation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Westminster_Theological_Seminary&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Accreditation")]

Westminster, under a [charter](/wiki/University_charter "University charter") from the [Commonwealth of Pennsylvania](/wiki/Commonwealth_of_Pennsylvania "Commonwealth of Pennsylvania") granted in 1930 and as subsequently amended, has the power to grant the degrees of Master of Arts in Counseling, Master of Arts (Religion), Master of Divinity, Master of Theology, Doctor of Ministry, and Doctor of Philosophy.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Since 1954, Westminster has been [accredited](/wiki/Higher_education_accreditation_in_the_United_States "Higher education accreditation in the United States") by the [Middle States Commission on Higher Education](/wiki/Middle_States_Commission_on_Higher_Education "Middle States Commission on Higher Education") or its predecessor.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) It is also accredited by the [Association of Theological Schools in the United States and Canada](/wiki/Association_of_Theological_Schools_in_the_United_States_and_Canada "Association of Theological Schools in the United States and Canada").[[19]](#cite_note-ATS-19)

## Academics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Westminster_Theological_Seminary&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Academics")]

The seminary currently offers the following degrees:[[19]](#cite_note-ATS-19)[[20]](#cite_note-programs-20)

* [Master of Arts](/wiki/Master_of_Arts "Master of Arts") (MA)
* [Master of Divinity](/wiki/Master_of_Divinity "Master of Divinity") (M.Div.)
* [Master of Theology](/wiki/Master_of_Theology "Master of Theology") (Th.M.)
* [Doctor of Ministry](/wiki/Doctor_of_Ministry "Doctor of Ministry") (D.Min.)
* [Doctor of Philosophy](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy") (Ph.D.)

Westminster publishes the semi-annual *[Westminster Theological Journal](/wiki/Westminster_Theological_Journal "Westminster Theological Journal")*.[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Westminster_Theological_Seminary&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Legacy")]

According to [Roger E. Olson](/wiki/Roger_E._Olson "Roger E. Olson"), Westminster has had an influence on [evangelicalism](/wiki/Evangelicalism "Evangelicalism") far beyond its size.[[23]](#cite_note-23) Beyond Westminster's impact in the theological realm, the pioneering work of [J. Alan Groves](/wiki/J._Alan_Groves "J. Alan Groves") and his students has produced the codification of the [Westminster Leningrad Codex](/wiki/Westminster_Leningrad_Codex "Westminster Leningrad Codex"), which underlies all modern [Bible software](/wiki/Bible_software "Bible software").[[24]](#cite_note-DeRose-24)
